A car, also known as an automobile, is a wheeled motor vehicle designed primarily for the transportation of people. Most cars have four wheels and are designed to run primarily on roads. They typically have seating for one to eight people, depending on the model and design. Most cars are powered by internal combustion engines, although electric cars, which use electric motors and batteries, have become increasingly popular. Cars typically include a variety of features for the comfort, convenience, and safety of passengers, including seats, seat belts, airbags, air conditioning, sound systems, navigation systems, and more. The design and features of a car can vary widely based on its intended use, ranging from compact cars for city driving to large SUVs for off-road or family use. In addition to their primary function of providing transport, cars have significant cultural, economic, and environmental impacts. They play an essential role in modern society, influencing urban planning, lifestyle, and industry.
